description: Syphir Shield threat intelligence — known malicious IPs, C2 servers, attack patterns, and malicious domains. Updated periodically. On the Pi this pulls from live threat feeds.

malicious_ip_ranges.label: Telegram infrastructure — monitor for C2 abuse

known_c2_domains.label: GitHub raw — common malware payload delivery
known_c2_domains.notes: Legitimate but heavily abused for malware staging. Flag unusual access patterns.

known_c2_domains.label: Pastebin — C2 config hosting
known_c2_domains.notes: Attackers host C2 configs here. Cross-reference with volume.

known_c2_domains.label: Telegram Bot API — C2 channel
known_c2_domains.notes: Increasingly used as C2 channel. Bots can exfiltrate data and receive commands.

known_c2_domains.label: Discord API — C2 channel
known_c2_domains.notes: Discord webhooks used for C2 and data exfiltration.

known_c2_domains.label: ngrok — reverse tunnel C2
known_c2_domains.notes: Primary tool for establishing reverse shells through firewalls.

known_c2_domains.label: Burp Collaborator — pentesting C2
known_c2_domains.notes: Legitimate pentesting tool — flag if not expected

attack_signatures.name: Port scan — horizontal
attack_signatures.description: Single source IP connecting to many different destination IPs on the same port. Classic network reconnaissance.

attack_signatures.name: Port scan — vertical
attack_signatures.description: Single source IP connecting to many ports on the same destination. Service enumeration.

attack_signatures.name: Brute force — SSH
attack_signatures.description: Repeated connection attempts to port 22. Password spraying or credential stuffing.

attack_signatures.name: Brute force — RDP
attack_signatures.description: Repeated connection attempts to port 3389. Common Windows remote desktop attack.

attack_signatures.name: Lateral movement — internal SSH
attack_signatures.description: Device on LAN attempting SSH connections to other LAN devices. Possible worm or insider threat.

attack_signatures.name: C2 beacon — regular interval
attack_signatures.description: Device making connections to the same external IP at very regular intervals. Classic C2 beaconing.

attack_signatures.name: DNS tunneling
attack_signatures.description: Unusually high volume of DNS queries or very long DNS hostnames. Data exfiltration via DNS.

attack_signatures.name: Reverse shell attempt
attack_signatures.description: Outbound connection on unusual high port after receiving inbound packet. Possible reverse shell.

attack_signatures.name: Data exfiltration — bulk outbound
attack_signatures.description: Single device sending unusually large volume outbound in a short window. Possible bulk data theft.

attack_signatures.name: After-hours network activity
attack_signatures.description: Device generating significant network traffic outside business hours when office should be empty.

attack_signatures.name: New device on network
attack_signatures.description: Unknown MAC/IP address appeared on the network. Could be unauthorized device or attacker pivot.

attack_signatures.name: Connection to known C2
attack_signatures.description: Device connecting to a known command and control domain or IP range.

alert.description: Log incident and notify admin. No blocking.

block_and_alert.description: Block the source IP and notify admin.

isolate_and_alert.description: Isolate the affected LAN device and notify admin immediately.
